rows.definition: The default softmax scaled-dot-product self-attention token mixer (the be.Attention HAL op and m.attnSeq body) that mixes tokens via Q·K softmax over V.
rows.distinction: Unlike linear attention (Gated-DeltaNet), it computes an explicit O(n^2) softmax over key positions rather than a recurrent linear scan.

rows.definition: The self-attention sublayer's canonical tensor namespace (self_attn.{q,k,v,o}_proj plus q_norm/k_norm) that holds the projection weights of a softmax-attention block.
rows.distinction: It is the standard self-attention block, NOT the linear_attn token-mixer namespace used by Gated-DeltaNet layers.

rows.definition: The per-head context-vector buffer (nH*hd wide) that attention accumulates per position and then feeds into the o_proj output matmul.
rows.distinction: It is the dense-attention output buffer, NOT GLM-DSA's sparse-attend concatenation attnConcat.

rows.definition: GLM-DSA's per-head sparse-attention output, concatenated across heads (nH*vHead) and fed into o_proj in the DeepSeek-style sparse-attend path.
rows.distinction: It is the GLM-DSA sparse path's output concat, NOT the dense attnOut buffer used by standard GQA attention.

rows.definition: The GGUF `attn_q` query-projection weight (maps hidden state to the per-head query vectors of a self-attention layer).
rows.distinction: It is the dense query projection, NOT the key projection attn_k.

rows.definition: The GGUF `attn_k` key-projection weight (maps hidden state to the per-KV-head key vectors of a self-attention layer).
rows.distinction: It is the dense key projection, NOT the MLA-absorbed key up-projection attn_k_b.

rows.definition: The GLM-DSA/MLA absorbed key up-projection weight `attn_k_b.weight` ([n_head, kv_lora, qk_nope], transposed at convert time) that lifts the compressed KV latent to per-head keys.
rows.distinction: It is the latent-to-head MLA up-projection, NOT the dense full-rank key projection attn_k.

rows.definition: The GLM-DSA/MLA value up-projection weight `attn_v_b.weight` ([n_head, v_head, kv_lora]) that lifts the compressed KV latent to per-head value vectors.
rows.distinction: It is the value-side MLA up-projection, NOT the key-side absorbed up-projection attn_k_b.

rows.definition: The single fused query+key+value projection tensor (self_attn.qkv_proj / GGUF attn_qkv) that some families (Phi, Qwen3.5 linear) ship and fak splits into q/k/v at load.
rows.distinction: It is one packed projection, NOT the separate attn_q / attn_k / attn_v weights.

rows.definition: cfg.attnScale() — the per-head scaled-dot-product denominator (1/sqrt(HeadDim), or 1/sqrt(QueryPreAttnScalar) for Gemma) multiplied into the raw QK scores before softmax.
rows.distinction: It scales scores PRE-softmax, NOT the Gemma2 tanh logit soft-cap applied to the scores.

rows.definition: cfg.AttnSoftcap — the Gemma2 tanh soft-cap value applied in-place to attention logits (softcapInPlace) to bound them; 0 means off.
rows.distinction: It bounds the score magnitude via tanh, NOT the SDPA scale denominator attnScale.

rows.definition: cfg.AttentionBias — the all-or-nothing flag (inferred from attn_{q,k,v}.bias tensors) that the q/k/v projections carry an additive learned bias (e.g. Qwen2).
rows.distinction: It is a learned projection bias on q/k/v, NOT the per-score soft-cap AttnSoftcap.

rows.definition: m.attentionNorms(l) — the bundle of pre/post normalization weights (and biases) that wrap the attention sublayer for a layer's topology.
rows.distinction: It is the attention sublayer's own norm weights, NOT the post_attention_layernorm that feeds the following MLP.

rows.definition: The HF-named `post_attention_layernorm.weight` — the RMSNorm applied to the residual BEFORE the MLP/SwiGLU sublayer (its name notwithstanding).
rows.distinction: Despite the name it normalizes the MLP input, NOT the attention sublayer's pre-norm (attentionNorms.pre).

rows.definition: m.attnSeq — the cacheless whole-sequence dense attention body (Q/K/V projection, RoPE, SDPA, o_proj) run over a panel of normalized inputs.
rows.distinction: It processes a whole sequence panel, NOT the cached single-position attnBody used on the decode/session path.

rows.definition: The attnBody closure — the per-step attention sublayer over an already-normalized input that reads/writes the KV cache (kv.go, glm_dsa_session, minimax_m3_session) and returns the raw attention output.
rows.distinction: It is the cached single-step sublayer, NOT the cacheless whole-sequence attnSeq.

rows.definition: attnPrefillInto — the batched causal GQA attention kernel that computes a P-token prefill panel's attention and writes it into attnOut with optional sliding-window and soft-cap.
rows.distinction: It serves a prefill panel, NOT one batched decode step across users (attnDecodeBatch).

rows.definition: attnDecodeBatch — computes per-user causal attention for ONE batched decode step across B users, bit-identical to serial Step.
rows.distinction: It is the single-step decode analogue, NOT the prefill-panel kernel attnPrefillInto.

rows.definition: attnGQAFuse — the env-tunable flag that fuses a GQA group of 3 query heads sharing a KV head into one combined score-dot fast path.
rows.distinction: It is the GQA-group fusion toggle, NOT the SIMD score-dot enable attnFdot3SIMD it depends on.

rows.definition: attnFdot3SIMD (with its attnFdot3SIMDMinBatch threshold) — the toggle enabling a 3-wide SIMD fused score dot-product (scoreDot3) for a GQA-3 group.
rows.distinction: It vectorizes the QK score dot-product, NOT the value-accumulation saxpy path (attnSaxpy3SIMD).

rows.definition: attnSaxpy3SIMDMinBatch / attnSaxpy3SIMDMinPos — the batch- and position-count thresholds gating the 3-wide SIMD saxpy fast path that accumulates weighted value vectors.
rows.distinction: These gate the value-accumulation saxpy, NOT the score dot-product path attnFdot3SIMD.

rows.definition: The Gated-DeltaNet linear-attention token mixer that replaces softmax attention on hybrid layers (selected by c.isLinearAttnLayer); a recurrent linear scan instead of an O(n^2) softmax.
rows.distinction: It is the recurrent linear mixer, NOT the full softmax Attention token-mixer.

rows.definition: m.linearAttnSeq — the concrete Gated-DeltaNet recurrent-scan implementation over a sequence (with linearAttnSeqBatched as the issue-#443 batched variant) that produces the linear-attention outputs.
rows.distinction: It is the scan computation, NOT the linear-attention layer concept/predicate (linearattn) nor its state cache (linear-attn-cache).

rows.definition: cfg.FullAttentionInterval — the hybrid Qwen3.5 schedule where every Nth decoder layer uses full (global) attention (LayerTypes 'full_attention') while the rest slide.
rows.distinction: It marks the global-attention layers in the cadence, NOT a sliding-window layer.

rows.definition: The 'sliding_attention' LayerTypes value (derived in deriveLayerAttentionAxes) marking a layer that attends only within a bounded sliding window (SWA).
rows.distinction: It is a windowed-attention layer, NOT a full/global-attention layer in the FullAttentionInterval cadence.

rows.definition: c.isMiniMaxSparseAttn — the predicate selecting the MiniMax-M3 sparse-attention decoder path (m.layerMiniMax) for a layer.
rows.distinction: It routes to MiniMax sparse attention, NOT the Gated-DeltaNet linear-attention path.

rows.definition: The SGLang-style RadixAttention prefix KV-cache (internal/radixkv) that walks a radix tree of past requests and reuses the longest cached prompt prefix.
rows.distinction: It reuses shared prompt PREFIXES, NOT the PagedAttention scheme of paging a single sequence's KV into fixed blocks.

rows.definition: The vLLM-style PagedAttention / flash fused attention kernel concept that fak's Caps.FusedAttn can lower the whole Attention op to (paged KV blocks, online softmax).
rows.distinction: It is the paged/flash kernel for one sequence's KV, NOT RadixAttention's cross-request prefix reuse.

rows.definition: Caps.FusedAttn — the backend capability bit advertising it can lower the Attention op to ONE fused flash/online-softmax (paged) kernel (e.g. the CUDA backend, issue #486).
rows.distinction: It is the capability seam the model loop type-asserts, NOT the kernel concept itself (PagedAttention).

rows.definition: c.ropeAttentionFactor() — the RoPE long-context attention-scaling multiplier (YaRN/longrope), read from RopeParams.AttentionFactor, applied to the attention computation.
rows.distinction: It scales for extended context via RoPE, NOT the base per-head SDPA denominator attnScale.

rows.definition: m.softmaxAttentionScores — applies softmax to a head's attention scores, honoring per-head attention-sink logits (self_attn.sinks) when present.
rows.distinction: It is the post-score normalization step, NOT the pre-softmax scale attnScale.

rows.definition: cachemeta.AttentionIndexReferences — the predicate reporting whether an attention_index cache entry depends on a given KV span (used by eviction/external-invalidation).
rows.distinction: It tests a dependency on the index, NOT the attention_index entry/request itself (attention-index).

rows.definition: ctxplan.SignalNoiseFromAttention — folds a Plan plus a WITNESSED per-span attention attribution into a signal-vs-noise mass split for a turn.
rows.distinction: It yields the signal/noise scalar split, NOT the per-turn bucketed Outcome (OutcomeFromAttention).

rows.definition: ctxplan.OutcomeFromAttention — produces a WITNESSED per-turn Outcome (classifies each resident span by its witnessed attention mass vs a hit threshold).
rows.distinction: It classifies spans into outcome buckets, NOT the signal/noise scalar split (SignalNoiseFromAttention).

rows.definition: The cmd/attnsnrsi command that closes the attention signal/noise RSI loop in SHADOW (#867), replaying a session's Forecast scored on attention attribution through rsiloop.
rows.distinction: It is the RSI driver command, NOT the scoring function SignalNoiseFromAttention it consumes.

rows.definition: m.SetAttnObserver — installs (or clears with nil) the default-off attention-mass witness observer on the model, used to capture post-softmax weights without perturbing the forward pass.
rows.distinction: It is the installer, NOT the AttnObserver callback type it installs (attn-observer).

rows.definition: emitAttnRow — the hot-path helper that copies the post-softmax weights for one (layer, query position, head) window and hands the fresh slices to the installed observer.
rows.distinction: It is the per-row emit site, NOT the installer SetAttnObserver or the observer type attn-observer.
